justification: All five rubric criteria met: (1) Audit log gap clearly identified — 'logs record THAT an action happened but not WHAT action or against WHOM', missing operation type, target user/account ID, parameters, and outcome. (2) PII-in-logs risk identified — customer name, address, card last-4 in application logs flagged as compliance violation and data breach risk. (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure — 'leaks class names, library versions, file paths' to attackers. (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified — explicitly called out as enabling credential stuffing, data exfiltration, and mass fraud. (5) Public internet exposure escalated appropriately via Coach K to MJ (architecture) and Bird (compliance), not unilaterally mandated. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Audit log gap thoroughly identified with specific missing fields: operation type, target user/account, parameters, outcome, before/after state
PII-in-logs risk correctly identified with PCI-DSS implications and third-party log aggregator data breach scenario
Stack trace information disclosure clearly flagged — 'class names, library versions, internal paths — an attacker's roadmap'
Rate limiting absence identified with concrete attack scenarios: credential stuffing, data exfiltration, mass refunds, resource exhaustion
Public internet exposure escalated to Coach K → MJ + Bird, not unilaterally mandated — proper escalation discipline
RBAC absence escalated to Bird for domain rules rather than Pippen mandating it directly — correct boundary awareness
Health check stub identified as 'worse than no health check — it actively lies to your load balancer'
Exceptional depth on compound risk scenarios — e.g., 'public internet + no RBAC + no rate limiting + insufficient audit logging' creating undetectable unlimited access
Minor: RBAC listed as P0 blocker (#2) which borders on Pippen mandating a product/security team decision, though this is mitigated by the escalation to Bird for role definitions
